---
title: Portainer
description: Visual Docker management — containers, images, volumes, networks, and stacks.
---

import Mermaid from '../../../components/Mermaid.astro';

**Image:** `portainer/portainer-ce:2.21.4`  
**Ports:** `9443` (HTTPS UI), `8000` (edge agent tunnel)  
**Data:** `portainer_data` (named volume)

## Introduction

[Portainer Community Edition](https://www.portainer.io/) is a lightweight web UI for managing Docker resources. It provides a visual interface for containers, images, volumes, networks, and stacks — including deploying templates from this repo as stacks without using the command line.

## When to Use

- You want to manage Docker containers visually without remembering CLI commands
- You want to deploy services from this repo via a web UI instead of `docker compose`
- You manage multiple Docker hosts and want a single dashboard
- You want to onboard non-technical team members to your self-hosted infrastructure

## Architecture

<Mermaid diagram={`
graph LR
    Browser["Browser"] -->|":9443 HTTPS"| Portainer
    EdgeAgent["Remote Docker host\n(edge agent)"] -->|":8000 tunnel"| Portainer
    subgraph Portainer["Portainer Container"]
        UI["Web UI"]
        Data["portainer_data volume"]
    end
    Portainer -->|"Docker socket"| DockerSocket["/var/run/docker.sock"]
    DockerSocket --> LocalDocker["Local Docker Engine\n(containers, volumes, networks)"]
`} />

## Prerequisites

- Docker Engine 20.10+, Docker Compose v2
- Docker socket available on host

## Ports

| Port | Protocol | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `9443` | HTTPS | Portainer web UI (self-signed cert by default) |
| `8000` | TCP | Edge agent tunnel for remote environments |

Configurable via `PORTAINER_HTTPS_PORT` and `PORTAINER_TUNNEL_PORT`.

Portainer's container also listens on internal port `9000` (plain HTTP), which is **not** published to the host and has no corresponding env var. It exists for reverse proxies on the same Docker network to reach Portainer without TLS — see [Integration](#integration) below.

## Volumes

| Volume | Contents |
|---|---|
| `portainer_data` | Settings, users, endpoints, stack definitions |

## Configuration

```bash
cd portainer
cp .env.example .env
```

| Variable | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `PORTAINER_HTTPS_PORT` | `9443` | HTTPS UI port |
| `PORTAINER_TUNNEL_PORT` | `8000` | Edge agent tunnel port |
| `PORTAINER_ADMIN_PASSWORD_HASH` | _(empty)_ | Pre-set admin password (bcrypt hash). If unset, set via first-access UI |

**Pre-setting an admin password (optional):**
```bash
docker run --rm httpd:2.4-alpine htpasswd -nbB admin 'your-password' | cut -d ':' -f2
# Paste the output as PORTAINER_ADMIN_PASSWORD_HASH in .env
```

## Workflow

```bash
# Start
docker compose up -d

# Open UI immediately — you have 5 minutes to set an admin password
open https://localhost:9443
# Accept the self-signed certificate warning

# Stop
docker compose down

# Stop and remove all data (settings, stacks, endpoints) — destructive
docker compose down -v
```

:::danger[5-minute timeout]
If you don't set an admin password within **5 minutes** of first start, Portainer locks itself for security. Fix: `docker compose restart`, then set the password immediately.
:::

## Use Cases

**Deploy this repo's templates as stacks:**
1. **Stacks → Add Stack**
2. Choose **Repository** and enter `https://github.com/gmbalaa14/docker-templates`
3. Set the **Compose path** to `<template-name>/docker-compose.yml`
4. Add environment variables in the **Environment variables** section

**Manage running containers:**
Navigate to **Containers** to start, stop, restart, inspect logs, or exec into any container.

**Browse volumes and networks:**
Navigate to **Volumes** or **Networks** to inspect, remove, or create Docker resources.

**Multi-host management:**
Add remote Docker hosts as **Environments** using the edge agent (port 8000).

## Security

:::caution[Docker socket]
The Docker socket mount gives Portainer root-equivalent access to the host. Secure Portainer's admin account with a strong password and restrict access to the port.
:::

## Integration

### Nginx Proxy Manager

Uncomment the [NPM opt-in block](/networking/#the-npm-opt-in-pattern) in `docker-compose.yml`, restart, and create a proxy host: `portainer.yourdomain.com → portainer:9000`.

Note: If routing through NPM, set NPM to forward to port `9000` (Portainer's internal HTTP port, see [Ports](#ports)) rather than `9443`, and let NPM handle SSL termination.
